steps.title: Identify the organizing principle
steps.content: The passage explicitly opens with a single, definitive statement: silversword species “collectively illustrate the process of adaptive radiation.” This assertion anchors the entire passage—all subsequent details about their physical diversity, common ancestry, and habitat adaptation serve as evidence for this central idea.

steps.title: Match the main idea to the choices
steps.content: Choice D directly states this core claim: silverswords are good examples of adaptive radiation on the Hawaiian archipelago. The other three fail to capture the passage's focus—A contradicts the text (claiming similarity when it emphasizes distinct forms), B describes secondary details about the archipelago rather than what silverswords exemplify, and C overgeneralizes by claiming most plants when only silverswords are discussed. That matches choice D — D is correct.
